convertionPbacGuide.title: How to Use PBAC After Conversion
convertionPbacGuide.intro: PBAC controls parent menu visibility (access levels) and submenu/action permissions. Use the provided helpers and access map to manage visibility and route protection.
steps: Edit src/templates/sidebar.php and wrap the parent menu with a parent guard: <?php if (has_menu_access('Maintenance')): ?> ... <?php endif; ?>

Protect each submenu link with has_permission('MenuLabel SubmenuLabel'). Example:
  • Access Level

Keep permission keys stable. The default generator uses the format 'MenuLabel SubmenuLabel' (for example 'Maintenance Access Level') — use the same string when calling has_permission() and when assigning permissions to users.

Run 'ml gen' (or 'php tools/generate_access_map.php') from your project root to generate src/assets/js/accesslevel-map.json. This file maps menus, submenu permission ids, and access_level bitmasks used by the admin UI.

Use the Access Level page (Maintenance → Access Level) to assign an access_level and a set of permission ids to a user. At login the PBAC session loader populates $_SESSION['user_access_level'] and $_SESSION['user_permissions'].

To protect routes or controllers use require_mapped_permission_for_current_route() or call has_permission('Permission Key') in your middleware/controllers. The scaffold also provides helpers like requirePermission() and requireMenuAccess().
Admin rule: an access_level of -1 grants all permissions. Admin rows with missing permissions may be auto-populated by the scaffold's session loader.
notes: Permission ID format: 'MenuLabel SubmenuLabel' (space-separated). The generator uses this convention — changing sidebar labels requires re-running 'ml gen' to keep the map in sync.
If 'ml gen' is not available, run tools/generate_access_map.php manually from the project root to produce src/assets/js/accesslevel-map.json.
Sidebar PHP snippet patterns are used by the map generator: ensure submenu link labels (span.sidebar__submenu-label) and href targets are correct so permissions and targets are extracted properly.

installAndUseClaudeCode.explanation: Install ML CLI first (main requirement for everything else). Use the command for your operating system.
installAndUseClaudeCode.expectedResult: ML CLI installed and available in PATH.

installAndUseClaudeCode.explanation: Install Free Claude Code with ML CLI. Run ml install ai to clone free-claude-code repo, install uv + Python 3.14, configure .env with default models, prompt for NVIDIA NIM API key, install Claude Code via npm, and patch VS Code settings.
installAndUseClaudeCode.expectedResult: Free Claude Code installed to C:\free-claude-code\free-claude-code with everything configured.

installAndUseClaudeCode.explanation: Sign up / log in at https://build.nvidia.com/ and copy your API key. During install you will be prompted to enter it.
installAndUseClaudeCode.expectedResult: NVIDIA NIM API key is stored in C:\free-claude-code\free-claude-code\.env.

installAndUseClaudeCode.explanation: Start uvicorn (API server on port 8082) and Claude Code in visible terminal windows. Uvicorn runs in free-claude-code directory; Claude Code inherits your current working directory.
installAndUseClaudeCode.expectedResult: Two PowerShell windows open — one for uvicorn, one for Claude Code.

installAndUseClaudeCode.explanation: Start uvicorn in background and Claude Code visibly in your project directory. Use this when you are already inside your project folder and want Claude Code to start there instead of free-claude-code.
installAndUseClaudeCode.expectedResult: Uvicorn runs silently in background on port 8082; Claude Code window opens in your current directory.

installAndUseClaudeCode.explanation: Change the active AI model. Select Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, or the default model tier and enter a nvidia_nim model slug.
installAndUseClaudeCode.expectedResult: .env updated with the new model. Restart with ml --ai restart to load it.

installAndUseClaudeCode.explanation: Stop and restart both processes (e.g., after changing models or API key in .env).
installAndUseClaudeCode.expectedResult: Fresh uvicorn + Claude Code processes running with updated configuration.

installAndUseClaudeCode.explanation: Check for and pull the latest updates in the free-claude-code installation from the remote repository.
installAndUseClaudeCode.expectedResult: Git pull fetches latest changes from the remote repository.

installAndUseClaudeCode.explanation: Update the NVIDIA NIM API key stored in .env for Free Claude Code authentication.
installAndUseClaudeCode.expectedResult: Interactive prompt for new API key. Value written to .env. Restart with ml --ai restart.

installAndUseClaudeCodeGuide.title: How to Use Free Claude Code with ML CLI
installAndUseClaudeCodeGuide.intro: Free Claude Code is a full-stack AI setup powered by NVIDIA NIM models. ML CLI is the main requirement — install ML CLI first, then use it to run ml install ai. The stack includes a uvicorn API server on port 8082 as the proxy to NVIDIA NIM models, while Claude Code connects to it as the AI engine. Use ml --ai commands to manage the stack.
steps: Run ml install ai to set up the entire stack: uv, Python 3.14, free-claude-code repo, Claude Code npm package, and VS Code configuration.
Get an NVIDIA NIM API key from https://build.nvidia.com/ — enter it when prompted by the installer or later with ml --ai key.

Default models are pre-configured on install: MODEL_OPUS=nvidia_nim/deepseek-ai/deepseek-v4-pro, MODEL_SONNET=nvidia_nim/minimaxai/minimax-m2.7, MODEL_HAIKU=nvidia_nim/z-ai/glm4.7, MODEL=nvidia_nim/z-ai/glm-5.1.
Run ml --ai to start uvicorn and Claude Code in visible terminal windows. Both processes must be running for Claude Code to work.
Run ml --ai claude from your project folder when you want Claude Code to start in that directory instead of free-claude-code. Uvicorn stays in the background.
Run ml --ai bg when you want both processes running silently with no open terminal windows.
To update free-claude-code to the latest version: ml --ai update, which pulls changes from the remote repository.
To switch models: ml --ai cm, pick a tier, enter the model slug. Then ml --ai restart for changes to take effect.
To update your API key: ml --ai key, paste the new key. Then ml --ai restart.
Shut everything down with ml --ai stop when you are done. Use ml --ai restart to reload after any .env changes.
Claude Code runs in your terminal — you can ask it to read files, write code, explain functions, run commands, and more using natural language.
notes: Uvicorn must be running before Claude Code — ml --ai starts uvicorn first, then Claude Code. ml --ai claude starts uvicorn in background first, then Claude Code.
Claude Code in ml --ai uses your current working directory. Navigate to your project first with ml nav --my-project before running ml --ai claude.
Models are stored in C:\free-claude-code\free-claude-code\.env. You can edit this file directly or use ml --ai cm.
Get a fresh NVIDIA NIM API key at https://build.nvidia.com/ if yours expires or hits rate limits.
The VS Code extension for Claude Code connects to the same uvicorn server started by ml --ai.

generalUsage.expectedResult: Current directory is ready for project operations.

generalUsage.explanation: Create or scaffold your working app.
Preview the app in browser and continue coding.

commonMistakes: Running commands outside C:\xampp\htdocs project context.
Skipping ml test userdb before first run.
Ignoring version checks and using outdated CLI binaries.

scenarios.title: Creating your first project
steps: ml --v
ml nav
ml create my-app
ml nav --my-app
ml serve

scenarios.title: Workbench Export (Interactive)
steps: ml wb --export
Enter database names (comma-separated) or type all
For each selected database, enter table names or type all
Choose export method (1..6)
Enter folder name or press Enter for default
Confirm export
Check output in C:\ML CLI\Exports\<MM-DD-YYYY>\<FOLDER_NAME>\

scenarios.title: Apply PBAC Scaffold
steps: ml nav --<project_name>
ml create --pbac <project_name>
Confirm PBAC conversion prompt
ml gen
ml serve

scenarios.title: Configuring and running schema backups
steps: ml create --config
ml --b
ml --b userdb
ml --b all

scenarios.title: Fixing database connection issues
steps: ml test userdb
ml add userdb
ml test userdb

scenarios.title: Migrating to a decentralized database
steps: ml nav --<project_name>
ml migrate -db <DATABASE>
Confirm migration prompt with Y
Review migration-log.md for copied rows and rewritten files

scenarios.title: Starting development session
steps: ml --c
ml update (if needed)
ml nav --my-app
ml serve

scenarios.title: Recovering from setup errors
steps: ml --v
ml --c
ml update
ml test userdb

scenarios.title: Automated Scenario Example
steps: Run ml wb --export
Select DB names
Confirm export

scenarios.title: Serve Current Project (Local)
steps: ml serve
Opens http://localhost/<current_project> in browser

scenarios.title: Serve Current Project (Online)
steps: ml serve -o
Creates ngrok tunnel on 80 (fallback 8080)
Opens https://<ngrok-domain>/<current_project>

scenarios.title: Serve Specific Project (Local)
steps: ml serve --projectname
Opens http://localhost/<projectname> in browser

scenarios.title: Serve Specific Project (Online)
steps: ml serve --projectname -o
ml serve projectname -o
ml serve projectname --online
Creates ngrok tunnel on 80 (fallback 8080)
Opens https://<ngrok-domain>/<projectname>

scenarios.title: Stop Online Tunnel
steps: ml serve -o
Share link is created via ngrok
ml serve -stop

scenarios.title: Install Free Claude Code
steps: ml install ai
Enter your NVIDIA NIM API key when prompted (get from https://build.nvidia.com/)
Wait for prerequisites (uv, Python 3.14) to install
Claude Code installed via npm and VS Code settings configured

scenarios.title: Claude Code Only — Run from Your Project Directory
steps: ml nav --my-project
ml --ai claude
Uvicorn starts in background on port 8082
Claude Code window opens in your current project directory
Work on project files using natural language

scenarios.title: Switch AI Model
steps: ml --ai cm
Select model tier: 1=Opus, 2=Sonnet, 3=Haiku, 4=Default
Enter nvidia_nim model slug (e.g., minimaxai/minimax-m2.7)
ml --ai restart

scenarios.title: Update Free Claude Code
steps: ml --ai update
Git pull fetches and merges latest changes from remote repository
Restart services with ml --ai restart if needed

scenarios.title: Update NVIDIA API Key
steps: Get new key from https://build.nvidia.com/
ml --ai key
Paste the new NVIDIA_NIM_API_KEY
ml --ai restart

scenarios.title: Stop and Restart Free Claude Code
steps: ml --ai stop
ml --ai restart

errors.meaning: CLI not installed in PATH or shell session is stale.
errors.fix: Re-run installer, then open a new terminal and run ml --v.

errors.meaning: Network issue or blocked GitHub endpoint.
errors.fix: Check internet, proxy, and retry. Then run ml --c to validate connectivity.

errors.error: Failed to fetch remote updater script
errors.meaning: Cannot download the updater from GitHub.
errors.fix: Check DNS settings, firewall, and internet connection.
Check your internet and DNS settings.

errors.meaning: Schema or credentials are not ready.
errors.fix: Run ml add userdb then retry ml test userdb.

errors.meaning: Backup command ran before DB backup config was created.
errors.fix: Run ml create --config then retry ml --b.

errors.meaning: mysqldump is not installed or not detected from PATH/XAMPP.
errors.fix: Install MySQL client tools or set mysqldumpPath in ml create --config.

errors.meaning: Tried to run ml --ai but free-claude-code directory does not exist.
errors.fix: Run ml install ai to install Free Claude Code.

errors.meaning: PHP is not installed or not on PATH.
errors.fix: Install PHP and ensure it is accessible from command line.

errors.error: Failed to start Apache within timeout
errors.meaning: XAMPP Apache could not start automatically.
errors.fix: Start XAMPP Apache manually and retry ml serve.

errors.error: VSCode CLI (code) not found in PATH
errors.meaning: VS Code command line tools are not installed.
errors.fix: Install VS Code or add it to PATH. The CLI will still work without it.

errors.meaning: The project name specified does not exist in htdocs.
errors.fix: Check the project name or create it with ml create.

faq.q: How often should I run ml --c?
faq.a: Run it at the start of each dev session.

faq.q: Do I need to run ml update every time?
faq.a: Only when a newer version is reported.

faq.q: What is the fastest start command sequence?
How do I install Free Claude Code?
faq.a: First install ML CLI, then run ml install ai.

faq.q: Why should I use ml --ai instead of starting services manually?
faq.a: ml --ai automates starting uvicorn and Claude Code together, and manages them as a stack.

faq.q: What is the difference between ml --ai and ml --ai claude?
faq.a: ml --ai shows both windows. ml --ai claude starts uvicorn in background and Claude Code in your current directory.

faq.q: How do I update Free Claude Code?
faq.a: Run ml --ai update to pull the latest changes from the remote repository.

faq.q: Can I run ML CLI on Mac/Linux?

faq.a: Migrates userdb table structures and data to a decentralized target database.

faq.q: How do I change AI models?
faq.a: Run ml --ai cm, select a tier, enter a nvidia_nim model slug, then ml --ai restart.
Check if another service is using port 80, or start XAMPP Apache manually.

faq.q: How do I backup my database schemas?
faq.a: First run ml create --config to set up credentials, then ml --b or ml --b <schema>.
PBAC uses permission-based access with access levels. RBAC uses role-based access with user roles. PBAC is typically used for menu visibility.
